title: Raison et sensibilité, ou les deux manières d'aimer (Tome 2)
summaries: "Raison et sensibilité, ou les deux manières d'aimer (Tome 2)" by Jane Austen is a novel published in 1811. Two sisters navigate love and heartbreak in contrasting ways. Marianne Dashwood, passionate and romantic, openly pursues the charming Willoughby, while her elder sister Elinor conceals her feelings for Edward Ferrars. When betrayal strikes, Marianne must learn to overcome pain with her sister's help, as Elinor stoically sacrifices her dreams for family duty. The story explores the tension between individual passion and social stability. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
subjects: England -- Social life and customs -- 19th century -- Fiction
